breakdown: This PR widens the boolean acceptance semantics in _is_state_flag_true and _is_state_flag_false to accept LLM numeric booleans (int 1/0, string '1'/'0'). The type safety is excellent - the code correctly excludes bool from the int check using isinstance. Test coverage is comprehensive with 4 test methods covering accepted/rejected values and bool/int distinction. Documentation clearly explains the production bug (silent level-up stalls) that motivated the change.
key_changes: Add int 1 and '1' string to accepted values in _is_state_flag_true
Add int 0 and '0' string to accepted values in _is_state_flag_false
Add isinstance check to exclude bool from int handling (bool is subclass of int in Python)
Add comprehensive test class StateFlagSemanticsTest with 4 test methods
Update docstrings to explain LLM-generated state update use case and production bug being fixed
